title: Web crawler returns 0 pages for every URL
author: insujeon
body:
## Summary

The web crawler returns **0 pages for every crawl URL**, producing an empty `<source>` and reporting `Crawl complete. Pages crawled: 0. Failed URLs: 0`.

## Root cause

In `_worker`, the dequeued URL is added to `visited_urls` **before** the `_should_crawl_url(url)` check:

```python
# _worker (~L1855)
self.visited_urls.add(url)
...
if not self._should_crawl_url(url):   # re-check
    queue.task_done(); continue
```

But `_should_crawl_url` rejects already-visited URLs (~L1589):

```python
if url in self.visited_urls:
    return False
```

So every URL — including the seed — is marked visited and then immediately rejected by that guard. Nothing is ever fetched.

## Reproduce

```
onefilellm https://www.windmill.dev/docs/intro --crawl-max-depth 1 --crawl-max-pages 5
# -> Crawl complete. Pages crawled: 0. Failed URLs: 0
```

## Suggested fix

Mark the URL visited only **after** `_should_crawl_url` passes, keeping the `asyncio` lock around the visited check/add so concurrent workers don't double-fetch:

```python
if not self._should_crawl_url(url):
    queue.task_done(); continue

async with self._lock:
    if url in self.visited_urls:
        queue.task_done(); continue
    self.visited_urls.add(url)
```

With this change the same command crawls pages as expected.

## Related

Two pushed branches (`claude/code-review-bug-fixes-011CV6Hpt...`, `claude/review-issue-fix-01E3dM4vCi3...`) already reorder this, but they **drop the `asyncio` lock** around the visited check/add — reintroducing a concurrent double-fetch window — and are 1 commit behind `main`. The fix above keeps the lock.

title: SSRF Protection Bypass
author: RacerZ-fighting
body: ## Summary The URL safety validation in `extras/web_app.py` can be bypassed with a crafted URL that abuses parser differences around userinfo and backslash handling. The tested URL is: ```text http://127.0.0.1:6666\@arxiv.org ``` `urllib.parse.urlparse()` interprets the hostname as `arxiv.org`, so `_is_safe_url()` allows the request. However, downstream request execution can resolve the request target differently and reach `127.0.0.1:6666`, creating a server-side request forgery risk. ## Affected Component - Repo: https://github.com/jimmc414/onefilellm (commit 99c51a2cbe8cc01c0db037a9f800ca31fae9c2cd) - File: `extras/web_app.py` - Function: `_is_safe_url(url: str)` - Route: `POST /` - Downstream request paths: - `process_arxiv_pdf()` in `onefilellm.py` - `crawl_and_extract_text()` in `onefilellm.py` ## Vulnerability Type Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) ## Root Cause The safety check relies on `urllib.parse.urlparse(url).hostname` only. For the crafted URL: ```text http://127.0.0.1:6666\@arxiv.org ``` Python URL parsing treats the substring after `@` as the hostname: ```text hostname = arxiv.org ``` Because `arxiv.org` is not a private, loopback, link-local, or reserved IP address, `_is_safe_url()` returns `True`. The same raw URL is then passed into downstream request logic. The web application also uses substring checks such as: ```python elif "arxiv.org" in input_path: ``` This causes the crafted URL to enter the arXiv processing path even though the actual intended network target is controlled by the attacker-controlled prefix. ## Proof of Concept Input: ```text http://127.0.0.1:6666\@arxiv.org ``` Expected security behavior: The application should reject the URL because it contains a loopback target. Actual behavior: The safety check accepts the URL because `urlparse()` reports `arxiv.org` as the hostname. The application then proceeds into downstream request execution. ## Demo Code Save the following minimal request-flow demo as `ssrf_request_slice_demo.py`: ```python import argparse import ipaddress from urllib.parse import urlparse import requests def _is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool: """Reject URLs targeting private/internal networks.""" try: parsed = urlparse(url) hostname = parsed.hostname if not hostname: return False if hostname in ("localhost", "metadata.google.internal"): return False try: addr = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname) if addr.is_private or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_link_local or addr.is_reserved: return False except ValueError: pass return True except Exception: return False def request_web_url(input_url: str) -> requests.Response: return requests.get( input_url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"}, timeout=30, ) def request_arxiv_pdf(input_url: str) -> requests.Response: arxiv_clean_url = input_url.split("?")[0].split("#")[0] pdf_url = arxiv_clean_url.replace("/abs/", "/pdf/") + ".pdf" return requests.get(pdf_url, timeout=30) def process_input(input_url: str) -> requests.Response: parsed = urlparse(input_url) if parsed.scheme not in ("http", "https"): raise ValueError("Only http and https URLs are accepted by this demo.") if not _is_safe_url(input_url): raise ValueError("URL rejected by safety policy.") print("safe_url_check=True") print(f"parsed_hostname={urlparse(input_url).hostname}") if "arxiv.org" in input_url: print("route=arxiv") return request_arxiv_pdf(input_url) print("route=generic_web") return request_web_url(input_url) def main() -> int: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser( description="Run the reduced URL safety-check-to-requests.get flow." ) parser.add_argument("url", metavar="URL") args = parser.parse_args() response = process_input(args.url) print(f"final_url={response.url}") print(f"status_code={response.status_code}") print(f"content_type={response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')}") print(f"body_bytes={len(response.content)}") return 0 if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main()) ``` ## Reproduction Steps 1. Start a local listener on port `6666`. 2. Save the demo code above as `ssrf_request_slice_demo.py`. 3. Run the demo with the crafted URL: ```text python3 ssrf_request_slice_demo.py 'http://127.0.0.1:6666\@arxiv.org' ``` 4. Observe that the URL passes `_is_safe_url()`. 5. Observe an inbound request to the local listener. ## Evidence ## Security Impact An attacker who can submit URLs to the web interface may be able to make the server send HTTP requests to internal or loopback services. Depending on the deployment environment, this can expose: - Local administrative services bound to `127.0.0.1` - Internal HTTP APIs - Cloud metadata endpoints - Services reachable only from the server network This may lead to internal service discovery, sensitive data exposure, or interaction with privileged local services.

title: Missing Python module
author: tahayparker
body:
➜ pip install onefilellm
Defaulting to user installation because normal site-packages is not writeable
ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement onefilellm (from versions: none)
ERROR: No matching distribution found for onefilellm

https://pypi.org/search/?q=onefilellm

Does the onefilellm python module not exist?

title: CSS files are excluded from processing by is_allowed_filetype() function
author: 6IXTAVERN
body:
### 🐛 Issue: CSS files are excluded from processing by `is_allowed_filetype()` function

**Labels:** `bug`, `enhancement`, `file-processing`

---

#### ❌ Problem Description

When OneFileLLM recursively scans repository directories, CSS files (`.css`) are completely excluded from processing. The `is_allowed_filetype()` function returns `False` for `.css` extensions, preventing any analysis of CSS content.

#### 🔍 Steps to Reproduce

1. Process any repository containing CSS files:
```bash
python onefilellm.py https://github.com/muckSponge/MaterialFox
```

2. Check the output.xml file
3. **Result**: No CSS file content is present, even though the repository contains significant CSS code in `/chrome/` directory

#### 💡 Expected Behavior

CSS files should be treated as processable text files and included in the analysis, similar to how HTML, JavaScript, and other web-related files are processed.

#### 📋 Technical Details

The issue is in the `is_allowed_filetype()` function which uses an allowlist of file extensions. Currently, `.css` is not included in this allowlist.

**Suggested fix**: Add `.css` to the list of allowed file extensions in the `is_allowed_filetype()` function.

#### 🛠️ Workaround

Currently, users must manually modify the `is_allowed_filetype()` function to include `.css` extension.

title: YouTube changed its URL structure and this broke the ability to extract YT transcripts.
author: jimmc414
body:
Will fix and keep backwards compatibility with previous format.

example:

(work) c:\python\onefilellm>python c:\python\onefilellm\onefilellm.py
╭───────────── onefilellm - Content Aggregator ──────────────╮
│                                                            │
│                                                            │
│ Processes Inputs and Wraps Content in XML:                 │
│                                                            │
│ • Local folder path                                        │
│ • GitHub repository URL                                    │
│ • GitHub pull request URL (PR + Repo)                      │
│ • GitHub issue URL (Issue + Repo)                          │
│ • Documentation URL (Web Crawl)                            │
│ • YouTube video URL (Transcript)                           │
│ • ArXiv Paper URL (PDF Text)                               │
│ • DOI or PMID (via Sci-Hub, best effort)                   │
│ • Text from stdin (e.g., `cat file.txt | onefilellm -`)    │
│ • Text from clipboard (e.g., `onefilellm --clipboard`)     │
│                                                            │
│ Output is saved to file and copied to clipboard.           │
│ Content within XML tags remains unescaped for readability. │
│ Multiple inputs can be provided as command line arguments. │
│                                                            │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Enter the path, URL, or alias: https://www.youtube.com/live/czVBmqZP6Ss
Processing inputs... ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━   0% • 0/1 sources • -:--:--

**Could not extract YouTube video ID from URL: https://www.youtube.com/live/czVBmqZP6Ss**
Successfully processed: https://www.youtube.com/live/czVBmqZP6Ss
Processing inputs... ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━   0% • 0/1 sources • -:--:--

title: Processing all issues in a guthub repo
author: brendanvz
body:
Perhaps I'm not understanding the functionality correctly, I'm trying to catalog all issues, opened and closed and their comments for a repo.

When running

python onefilellm.py
https://github.com/jimmc414/onefilellm/issues

All the the code is included in the xml, but no issues.

How can I iterate and add all issues and their comments?

Thanks

title: lxml_html_clean module not found
author: gbrian
body:
After installing in a venv python3.11 got that error. 
Installed "lxml_html_clean" and it worked fine.

title: pip package
author: timbmg
body: Would be great if you could package this so it can be used directly in python

</source>

Linux umbrel 6.1.0-34-amd64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Debian 6.1.135-1 (2025-04-25) x86_64 GNU/Linux

title: Processing Error for every URL
author: emkman
body:
Fresh install and no matter what URL I enter, I get the same error:

```
An error occurred during processing:
╭─────────────────────────────── Traceback (most recent call last) ────────────────────────────────╮
│ /Users/emkman/dev/onefilellm/onefilellm.py:2280 in main                                          │
│                                                                                                  │
│   2277 │   │   │   if not outputs:                                                               │
│   2278 │   │   │   │   raise RuntimeError("No inputs were successfully processed.")              │
│   2279 │   │   │                                                                                 │
│ ❱ 2280 │   │   │   final_output = combine_xml_outputs(outputs)                                   │
│   2281 │   │   │                                                                                 │
│   2282 │   │   │   # --- Output Generation ---                                                   │
│   2283 │   │   │   if final_output is None:                                                      │
│                                                                                                  │
│ /Users/emkman/dev/onefilellm/onefilellm.py:1434 in combine_xml_outputs                           │
│                                                                                                  │
│   1431 │   # Add each source                                                                     │
│   1432 │   for output in outputs:                                                                │
│   1433 │   │   # Remove any XML declaration if present (rare but possible)                       │
│ ❱ 1434 │   │   output = re.sub(r'<\?xml[^>]+\?>', '', output).strip()                            │
│   1435 │   │   combined.append(output)                                                           │
│   1436 │                                                                                         │
│   1437 │   # Close the wrapper                                                                   │
│                                                                                                  │
│ /Users/emkman/.local/share/uv/python/cpython-3.12.10-macos-aarch64-none/lib/python3.12/re/__init │
│ __.py:186 in sub                                                                                 │
│                                                                                                  │
│   183 │   if a string, backslash escapes in it are processed.  If it is                          │
│   184 │   a callable, it's passed the Match object and must return                               │
│   185 │   a replacement string to be used."""                                                    │
│ ❱ 186 │   return _compile(pattern, flags).sub(repl, string, count)                               │
│   187                                                                                            │
│   188 def subn(pattern, repl, string, count=0, flags=0):                                         │
│   189 │   """Return a 2-tuple containing (new_string, number).                                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object, got 'coroutine'
Could not write partial output: expected string or bytes-like object, got 'coroutine'
sys:1: RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'process_input' was never awaited
RuntimeWarning: Enable tracemalloc to get the object allocation traceback
```

I've tried a few different versions of Python, they all get the same result. I get the following dependency warnings on startup:
```
/Users/emkman/dev/onefilellm/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/requests/__init__.py:109: RequestsDependencyWarning: urllib3 (1.26.20) or chardet (5.2.0)/charset_normalizer (2.0.12) doesn't match a supported version!
  warnings.warn(
/Users/emkman/dev/onefilellm/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/mistune.py:435: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\|'
  cells[i][c] = re.sub('\\\\\|', '|', cell)
```

title: Suggestion: add load_dotenv() in the main script, otherwise github token in .env is not recognizable
author: hfpmatheus
body:
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

title: PRs?
author: snth
body:
Nice tool. Thank you for creating it! It's one one of a few that supports interesting content types like arxiv papers.

Do you accept PRs for improving the CLI and ease of use?

title: Expose the max_depth variable as a CLI argument
author: snth
body: Don't really want to modify the code on an adhoc basis.

title: Support for output format other than XML
author: snth
body:
I see in the output.xml that you say that your evaluations showed XML performed best but markdown is also very popular.

I'm also not sure what the `-f markdown` option does then?

title: 'dict' object has no attribute 'text' with YouTube link
author: cyrilli
body:
With the following YouTube link from the unit test:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KZ_NlnmPQYk
I got:
```
<onefilellm_output>
<source type="youtube_transcript" url="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KZ_NlnmPQYk">
<error>'dict' object has no attribute 'text'</error>
</source>
</onefilellm_output>
```

title: ModuleNotFoundError
author: eddy147
body:
Hi,
I am on pop!_os 22.04 (basically ubuntu 22.04).
When I do

```
$ pip install -U -r requirements.txt
$ python onefilellm.py
```

I get:
`$ ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_sqlite3'`

So:
`$ sudo apt install libsqlite3-dev `


But:
```
$ python onefilellm.py
$ ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_sqlite3'
```

Have you any idea? Thx :-)
